title: discover_parallel_candidates data payload
description:
Structured payload for the `discover_parallel_candidates` tool — the v0.7 Discovery Engine. Returns machine-discovered comparative-religion parallels from the curated Research/ cluster corpus, with full provenance trace.

Discipline reversal: where `find_antediluvian_parallel` REQUIRES named scholarly attribution (no scholar, no result), this tool RETURNS candidates WITHOUT human-scholar validation — flagged explicitly as machine-discovered, with `discovered_by: 'ai_traversal'` and `validation_status: 'pending'`. The structural-reasoning trace explains WHY the engine surfaced this pair.

This is the inverse-discipline tier: provisional parallels for human-scholar validation. Each candidate carries a discovery_trace showing which briefs supported the comparison, which structural features matched, and what the suggested anchor-to-check would be.

Provenance is `source: local`, `endpoint: local:discoveredCandidatesIndex`. Underlying dataset curated via AI traversal of 24 research briefs + 3 datasets at cuneiform-mcp v0.7.0.

query.description: Echo of the input query, normalized.

min_confidence.description: Minimum confidence threshold. Default 0.3 — surfaces all confidently-scored candidates; raise to 0.6+ for only the strongest.

validation_status.description: Filter by validation status. Default `pending` (the new candidates queue). `validated` candidates have been moved to the `find_antediluvian_parallel` dataset.

results.description: Ranked discovery candidates, highest confidence first.

corpus_metadata.description: Information about the corpus the discovery engine traversed.

DiscoveryCandidate.description: A machine-discovered comparative-religion parallel candidate. Explicitly second-class until human-scholar validation.

entity_a.description: First entity in the proposed parallel. Order is not directional unless `transmission_direction` is specified.

entity_b.description: Second entity in the proposed parallel.

parallel_type.description: Kind of parallel detected. Same vocabulary as `find_antediluvian_parallel` plus `iconographic` for visual-form correspondences.

discovered_by.description: Always `ai_traversal` for candidates from this tool. Field exists to make machine-discovery vs human-scholarship distinction load-bearing in downstream display.

confidence_score.description: Engine's confidence in the candidate. Composite of structural-similarity, lexical-overlap, transmission-plausibility scores. NOT a probability estimate — a relative ranking signal.

validation_status.description: Human-review status. New candidates start `pending`. Validated candidates are typically promoted into `antediluvianParallels.json` with `[VERIFIED]` scholarly_attribution; rejected candidates are kept here with reason for negative-result preservation.

discovery_trace.description: The reasoning trace explaining why the engine surfaced this pair. Auditable: a human scholar can verify the steps.

supporting_briefs.description: Specific brief filenames from ~/Desktop/Research/ that contain the entity attestations or motif descriptions the engine relied on. E.g. `["Apkallu.md", "Erra_Epic.md", "Igigi.md"]`.

structural_features.description: Specific structural features matched between the entities. E.g. `["seven-count canonical set", "pre-flood timing", "divine-origin attribution", "antediluvian-wisdom transmission"]`.

lexical_overlap.description: Specific shared terms, epithets, or descriptors. Optional. E.g. `["seven", "divine warrior", "protective"]`.

transmission_route.description: Plausible historical/cultural route by which the two entities might be related. E.g. `Mesopotamian → Aramaic-Jewish via Babylonian-exile transmission`. Optional.

reasoning_summary.description: One-paragraph natural-language synthesis of why the engine flagged this pair. Up to ~500 chars.

suggested_anchor.description: Specific scholar/publication that a human reviewer should check to validate or reject this candidate. E.g. `Check Annus 2010 JSP 19.4 for Sebitti-Watchers structural inversion` or `Check Wiggermann 1992 for apkallū bucket-and-cone ↔ Ninhursag omega-symbol gesture parallel`. Optional but recommended for high-confidence candidates.

transmission_direction.description: How the engine hypothesizes the relationship. `structural_only` for cases where no direct transmission is claimed — only that the two entities share a structural pattern. Optional, default `structural_only`.

notes.description: Free-form note. Often used for caveats, related candidates, or 'see also' pointers. Up to ~400 chars.

name.description: Canonical name of the entity. E.g. `Sebitti`, `Seven Watchers (Asael, Shemyaza, ...)`, `Apkallū bucket-and-cone gesture`.

type.description: Kind of entity. Determines how the parallel should be reviewed.

primary_brief.description: Which brief in ~/Desktop/Research/ is the entity's primary source-of-attestation. E.g. `Erra_Epic.md` for Sebitti.

tradition.description: Cultural-linguistic tradition the entity belongs to. Determines transmission-plausibility scoring.
